questions.question: Explain the basic structure of exception handling in C++ using try-catch-throw.
keyPoints: try block contains code that might throw exception
catch block handles the exception
throw keyword is used to raise/throw an exception
Exception transfers control from throw to matching catch

questions.question: What is stack unwinding in C++? Explain with an example.
keyPoints: Process of destroying local objects as exception propagates
Called when exception thrown but not caught locally
Destructors of local objects are called during unwinding
Continues until exception is caught or program terminates

questions.question: Explain the exception hierarchy in C++ with examples of standard exception classes.
keyPoints: std::exception is base class
Two main branches: std::logic_error and std::runtime_error
logic_error: invalid_argument, out_of_range, length_error
runtime_error: runtime_error, overflow_error, underflow_error
bad_alloc, bad_cast are other important ones

questions.question: What is the purpose of the 'noexcept' specifier in C++? What happens if a noexcept function throws?
keyPoints: Declares that a function will not throw exceptions
Used for optimization - compiler can make assumptions
If noexcept function throws, std::terminate is called
Can be used with boolean parameter: noexcept(true/false)

questions.question: Explain why destructors should never throw exceptions in C++.
keyPoints: Destructors called during stack unwinding
If destructor throws during unwinding, std::terminate is called
No way to handle exception from destructor properly
Resource cleanup may be incomplete if exception escapes

questions.question: What is the difference between catching exceptions by value and by reference in C++?
keyPoints: Catch by value creates a copy of the exception
Catch by reference avoids copying, preserves polymorphism
Catching by value may cause object slicing
Always prefer catch by reference in practice

questions.question: Explain the rules for ordering catch blocks in C++ with an example.
keyPoints: More specific exceptions must come before general ones
Derived class exceptions should be caught before base class
catch(...) should be last if used
Incorrect order leads to compilation error or wrong handler

questions.question: Write a C++ program demonstrating exception handling with try-catch blocks. Include examples of throwing standard exceptions and custom exceptions. Also demonstrate the use of catch(...) and rethrowing exceptions.
keyPoints: Include try block with code that may throw
Show multiple catch blocks for different exception types
Demonstrate throwing std::runtime_error or std::invalid_argument
Show catch(...) for catching all exceptions
Demonstrate rethrowing using throw;
Proper exception handling and error messages

questions.question: Explain exception handling in constructors and destructors in C++. What are the special considerations? Discuss with examples.
keyPoints: Constructors can throw exceptions to indicate failure
When constructor throws, object is not created
Destructor is NOT called for failed construction
Destructors should never throw - leads to terminate
Use try-catch in constructor initializer list for member exceptions
Show example demonstrating these concepts

questions.question: Create a custom exception class in C++ derived from std::exception. Write a program that demonstrates its use with try-catch blocks, showing proper exception propagation and handling.
keyPoints: Derive custom class from std::exception
Override what() method to return descriptive message
Use custom exceptions in functions
Demonstrate catching both custom and standard exceptions
Show order of catch blocks handling both types
Proper program flow with exceptions

questions.question: What is exception safety? Discuss the different levels of exception safety guarantees with examples.
keyPoints: Exception safety guarantees program state after exceptions
No-throw guarantee: operations won't throw
Strong guarantee: operation succeeds or no change occurs
Basic guarantee: object remains in valid state
Show examples of each level
Discuss RAII for achieving exception safety
